L'évaluation de 1 608 instances révèle que les agents de recherche excèlent dans la synthèse des preuves mais peinent dans les environnements de recherche peu familiers et à déterminer quand arrêter la recherche.

de.title: Bewertung des Such-Agenten in einer parallelen Welt
de.summary: Mind-ParaWorld schlägt einen Benchmark-Framework vor, um Such-Agenten bei Fragen jenseits der Wissensgrenzen des Modells durch Synthese realistischer Szenarien und dynamischer Informationsbeschaffung zu bewerten. Die Bewertung von 1.608 Instanzen zeigt, dass Such-Agenten bei der Synthese von Evidenz hervorragen, aber bei unbekannten Suchumgebungen und der Bestimmung, wann die Suche beendet werden sollte, Schwierigkeiten haben.

es.title: Evaluación del Agente de Búsqueda en un Mundo Paralelo
es.summary: Mind-ParaWorld propone un marco de evaluación para evaluar agentes de búsqueda en preguntas más allá del conocimiento del modelo sintetizando escenarios realistas con recuperación de información dinámica. La evaluación de 1.608 instancias revela que los agentes de búsqueda destacan en la síntesis de evidencia pero luchan con entornos de búsqueda desconocidos y determinar cuándo dejar de buscar.

title: MOOSEnger -- a Domain-Specific AI Agent for the MOOSE Ecosystem
summary: MOOSEnger is a conversational AI agent for the MOOSE physics simulation framework that converts natural-language requests to valid input specifications using RAG and domain-aware parsing, achieving 93% execution pass rate compared to 8% for LLM-only baselines. The system combines dialogue with deterministic validation and iterative syntax repair.
content:
arXiv:2603.04756v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: MOOSEnger is a tool-enabled AI agent tailored to the Multiphysics Object-Oriented Simulation Environment (MOOSE). MOOSE cases are specified in HIT ".i" input files; the large object catalog and strict syntax make initial setup and debugging slow. MOOSEnger offers a conversational workflow that turns natural-language intent into runnable inputs by combining retrieval-augmented generation over curated docs/examples with deterministic, MOOSE-aware parsing, validation, and execution tools. A core-plus-domain architecture separates reusable agent infrastructure (configuration, registries, tool dispatch, retrieval services, persistence, and evaluation) from a MOOSE plugin that adds HIT-based parsing, syntax-preserving ingestion of input files, and domain-specific utilities for input repair and checking. An input precheck pipeline removes hidden formatting artifacts, fixes malformed HIT structure with a bounded grammar-constrained loop, and resolves invalid object types via similarity search over an application syntax registry. Inputs are then validated and optionally smoke-tested with the MOOSE runtime in the loop via an MCP-backed execution backend (with local fallback), translating solver diagnostics into iterative verify-and-correct updates. Built-in evaluation reports RAG metrics (faithfulness, relevancy, context precision/recall) and end-to-end success by actual execution. On a 125-prompt benchmark spanning diffusion, transient heat conduction, solid mechanics, porous flow, and incompressible Navier--Stokes, MOOSEnger achieves a 0.93 execution pass rate versus 0.08 for an LLM-only baseline.

zh.title: MOOSEnger——MOOSE生态系统的特定领域AI代理
zh.summary: MOOSEnger是MOOSE物理模拟框架的对话AI代理，使用RAG和领域感知解析将自然语言请求转换为有效的输入规范，达到93%的执行通过率，相比LLM基线的8%。该系统将对话与确定性验证和迭代语法修复相结合。

fr.title: MOOSEnger -- un Agent IA Spécifique au Domaine pour l'Écosystème MOOSE
fr.summary: MOOSEnger est un agent IA conversationnel pour le framework de simulation physique MOOSE qui convertit les demandes en langage naturel en spécifications d'entrée valides en utilisant RAG et l'analyse sensible au domaine, atteignant un taux de réussite d'exécution de 93% par rapport à 8% pour les lignes de base LLM uniquement. Le système combine le dialogue avec la validation déterministe et la réparation syntaxique itérative.

de.title: MOOSEnger -- ein domänenspezifischer KI-Agent für das MOOSE-Ökosystem
de.summary: MOOSEnger ist ein konversationeller KI-Agent für das MOOSE-Physik-Simulationsframework, das natürlichsprachliche Anfragen durch RAG und domänenbewusste Analyse in gültige Eingabespezifikationen umwandelt und eine Ausführungspass-Rate von 93% gegenüber 8% für nur LLM-Baselines erreicht. Das System kombiniert Dialoge mit deterministischer Validierung und iterativer Syntaxreparatur.

es.title: MOOSEnger -- Un Agente IA Específico del Dominio para el Ecosistema MOOSE
es.summary: MOOSEnger es un agente de IA conversacional para el marco de simulación de física MOOSE que convierte solicitudes en lenguaje natural a especificaciones de entrada válidas usando RAG y análisis consciente del dominio, logrando una tasa de aprobación de ejecución del 93% en comparación con el 8% para líneas de base solo LLM. El sistema combina diálogos con validación determinística y reparación sintáctica iterativa.

title: Breaking Contextual Inertia: Reinforcement Learning with Single-Turn Anchors for Stable Multi-Turn Interaction
summary: This paper identifies "Contextual Inertia," where LLMs fail to incorporate new information in multi-turn conversations, and proposes RLSTA, a training approach leveraging single-turn capabilities as anchors to stabilize multi-turn alignment. The method demonstrates strong cross-domain generalization without requiring external verifiers.
content:
arXiv:2603.04783v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While LLMs demonstrate strong reasoning capabilities when provided with full information in a single turn, they exhibit substantial vulnerability in multi-turn interactions. Specifically, when information is revealed incrementally or requires updates, models frequently fail to integrate new constraints, leading to a collapse in performance compared to their single-turn baselines. We term the root cause as \emph{Contextual Inertia}: a phenomenon where models rigidly adhere to previous reasoning traces. Even when users explicitly provide corrections or new data in later turns, the model ignores them, preferring to maintain consistency with its previous (incorrect) reasoning path. To address this, we introduce \textbf{R}einforcement \textbf{L}earning with \textbf{S}ingle-\textbf{T}urn \textbf{A}nchors (\textbf{RLSTA}), a generalizable training approach designed to stabilize multi-turn interaction across diverse scenarios and domains. RLSTA leverages the model's superior single-turn capabilities as stable internal anchors to provide reward signals. By aligning multi-turn responses with these anchors, RLSTA empowers models to break contextual inertia and self-calibrate their reasoning based on the latest information. Experiments show that RLSTA significantly outperforms standard fine-tuning and abstention-based methods. Notably, our method exhibits strong cross-domain generalization (e.g., math to code) and proves effective even without external verifiers, highlighting its potential for general-domain applications.

zh.title: 打破语境惯性：使用单轮锚点进行强化学习以实现稳定的多轮交互
zh.summary: 本文确定了"语境惯性"现象，其中大语言模型在多轮对话中无法整合新信息，并提出RLSTA，一种利用单轮能力作为锚点以稳定多轮对齐的训练方法。该方法在不需要外部验证者的情况下展示了强大的跨域泛化能力。

fr.title: Briser l'Inertie Contextuelle : Apprentissage par Renforcement avec Ancres Single-Turn pour une Interaction Multi-Turn Stable
fr.summary: Cet article identifie l'"Inertie Contextuelle", où les LLM échouent à incorporer les nouvelles informations dans les conversations multi-tours, et propose RLSTA, une approche d'entraînement exploitant les capacités single-turn comme ancres pour stabiliser l'alignement multi-tours. La méthode démontre une forte généralisation inter-domaines sans nécessiter de vérificateurs externes.

de.title: Kontextuelle Trägheit durchbrechen: Verstärktes Lernen mit Single-Turn-Ankern für stabile Multi-Turn-Interaktion
de.summary: Dieses Papier identifiziert "Kontextuelle Trägheit", bei der LLMs keine neuen Informationen in mehrteiligen Gesprächen einbeziehen, und schlägt RLSTA vor, einen Trainingsansatz, der Single-Turn-Fähigkeiten als Anker nutzt, um mehrteilige Ausrichtung zu stabilisieren. Die Methode zeigt starke domänenübergreifende Verallgemeinerung ohne externe Verifizierer.

es.title: Rompiendo la Inercia Contextual: Aprendizaje por Refuerzo con Anclas de Un Solo Turno para Interacción Multi-Turno Estable
es.summary: Este artículo identifica la "Inercia Contextual", donde los LLM no logran incorporar nueva información en conversaciones multi-turno, y propone RLSTA, un enfoque de entrenamiento que aprovecha capacidades de un solo turno como anclas para estabilizar el alineamiento multi-turno. El método demuestra una fuerte generalización entre dominios sin requerir verificadores externos.

title: Timer-S1: A Billion-Scale Time Series Foundation Model with Serial Scaling
summary: Timer-S1 is an 8.3B parameter mixture-of-experts time series model achieving state-of-the-art forecasting results through serial scaling of architecture, dataset (TimeBench with one trillion points), and training pipelines. The Serial-Token Prediction objective and post-training extensions enhance both short-term and long-context performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.04791v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce Timer-S1, a strong Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) time series foundation model with 8.3B total parameters, 0.75B activated parameters for each token, and a context length of 11.5K. To overcome the scalability bottleneck in existing pre-trained time series foundation models, we perform Serial Scaling in three dimensions: model architecture, dataset, and training pipeline. Timer-S1 integrates sparse TimeMoE blocks and generic TimeSTP blocks for Serial-Token Prediction (STP), a generic training objective that adheres to the serial nature of forecasting. The proposed paradigm introduces serial computations to improve long-term predictions while avoiding costly rolling-style inference and pronounced error accumulation in the standard next-token prediction. Pursuing a high-quality and unbiased training dataset, we curate TimeBench, a corpus with one trillion time points, and apply meticulous data augmentation to mitigate predictive bias. We further pioneer a post-training stage, including continued pre-training and long-context extension, to enhance short-term and long-context performance. Evaluated on the large-scale GIFT-Eval leaderboard, Timer-S1 achieves state-of-the-art forecasting performance, attaining the best MASE and CRPS scores as a pre-trained model. Timer-S1 will be released to facilitate further research.

zh.title: Timer-S1: 具有序列扩展的十亿规模时间序列基础模型
zh.summary: Timer-S1是一个83亿参数的混合专家时间序列模型，通过架构、数据集(TimeBench包含一万亿个数据点)和训练管道的序列扩展实现了最先进的预测结果。序列-令牌预测目标和后训练扩展增强了短期和长上下文性能。

fr.title: Timer-S1 : Un modèle de fondation de séries temporelles à l'échelle du milliard avec mise à l'échelle en série
fr.summary: Timer-S1 est un modèle de séries temporelles de 8,3 milliards de paramètres à mélange d'experts achievant des résultats de prévision dernier cri grâce à la mise à l'échelle en série de l'architecture, de l'ensemble de données (TimeBench avec un billion de points) et des pipelines d'entraînement. L'objectif de prédiction série-jeton et les extensions post-entraînement améliorent les performances à court terme et à long contexte.

de.title: Timer-S1: Ein billionenskaliges Zeitreihenfundamentalmodell mit serieller Skalierung
de.summary: Timer-S1 ist ein 8,3-Milliarden-Parameter-Mixture-of-Experts-Zeitreihenmodell, das durch serielle Skalierung der Architektur, des Datensatzes (TimeBench mit einer Billionen Punkten) und der Trainingspipelines hochmoderne Prognoseergebnisse erreicht. Das Serial-Token-Prediction-Ziel und Nachtrainingserweiterungen verbessern sowohl die kurzfristige als auch die langfristige Kontextleistung.

es.title: Timer-S1: Un modelo fundamental de series temporales a escala de mil millones con escalado en serie
es.summary: Timer-S1 es un modelo de series temporales de 8,3 mil millones de parámetros de mezcla de expertos que logra resultados de pronóstico de última generación mediante escalado en serie de la arquitectura, el conjunto de datos (TimeBench con un billón de puntos) y canalizaciones de capacitación. El objetivo de predicción serie-token y extensiones posteriores al entrenamiento mejoran el rendimiento de corto plazo y contexto largo.

title: EchoGuard: An Agentic Framework with Knowledge-Graph Memory for Detecting Manipulative Communication in Longitudinal Dialogue
summary: EchoGuard uses knowledge graphs as structured memory to detect manipulative communication patterns in longitudinal dialogue, combining episodic tracking of events and emotions with semantic pattern detection. The agentic framework generates targeted Socratic prompts grounded in detected manipulation to guide user self-awareness.
content:
arXiv:2603.04815v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Manipulative communication, such as gaslighting, guilt-tripping, and emotional coercion, is often difficult for individuals to recognize. Existing agentic AI systems lack the structured, longitudinal memory to track these subtle, context-dependent tactics, often failing due to limited context windows and catastrophic forgetting. We introduce EchoGuard, an agentic AI framework that addresses this gap by using a Knowledge Graph (KG) as the agent's core episodic and semantic memory. EchoGuard employs a structured Log-Analyze-Reflect loop: (1) users log interactions, which the agent structures as nodes and edges in a personal, episodic KG (capturing events, emotions, and speakers); (2) the system executes complex graph queries to detect six psychologically-grounded manipulation patterns (stored as a semantic KG); and (3) an LLM generates targeted Socratic prompts grounded by the subgraph of detected patterns, guiding users toward self-discovery. This framework demonstrates how the interplay between agentic architectures and Knowledge Graphs can empower individuals in recognizing manipulative communication while maintaining personal autonomy and safety. We present the theoretical foundation, framework design, a comprehensive evaluation strategy, and a vision to validate this approach.

zh.title: EchoGuard: 一个用于检测纵向对话中操纵性沟通的智能体框架与知识图谱记忆
zh.summary: EchoGuard使用知识图谱作为结构化记忆来检测纵向对话中的操纵性沟通模式，结合事件和情感的情节跟踪与语义模式检测。该智能体框架生成基于检测到的操纵的有针对性的苏格拉底式提示，以指导用户的自我意识。

fr.title: EchoGuard : Un cadre agentique avec mémoire de graphe de connaissances pour détecter la communication manipulatrice dans le dialogue longitudinal
fr.summary: EchoGuard utilise des graphes de connaissances comme mémoire structurée pour détecter les modèles de communication manipulatrice dans le dialogue longitudinal, combinant le suivi épisodique des événements et des émotions avec la détection de motifs sémantiques. Le cadre agentique génère des invites socratiques ciblées enracinées dans la manipulation détectée pour guider la conscience de soi de l'utilisateur.

de.title: EchoGuard: Ein agentenbasiertes Framework mit Wissensgraph-Gedächtnis zur Erkennung manipulativer Kommunikation im Langzeitdialog
de.summary: EchoGuard nutzt Wissensgraphen als strukturiertes Gedächtnis, um manipulative Kommunikationsmuster im Langzeitdialog zu erkennen und verbindet dabei episodisches Tracking von Ereignissen und Emotionen mit semantischer Mustererkennung. Das agentenbasierte Framework generiert zielgerichtete sokratische Fragen, die in erkannter Manipulation verankert sind, um das Selbstbewusstsein des Benutzers zu fördern.

es.title: EchoGuard: Un marco agencial con memoria de gráfico de conocimiento para detectar comunicación manipuladora en diálogos longitudinales
es.summary: EchoGuard utiliza gráficos de conocimiento como memoria estructurada para detectar patrones de comunicación manipuladora en diálogos longitudinales, combinando el seguimiento episódico de eventos y emociones con la detección de patrones semánticos. El marco agencial genera indicaciones socráticas dirigidas fundamentadas en la manipulación detectada para guiar la autoconciencia del usuario.

title: LLM-Grounded Explainability for Port Congestion Prediction via Temporal Graph Attention Networks
summary: AIS-TGNN couples temporal graph neural networks with LLM reasoning to predict port congestion while generating operationally interpretable explanations grounded in model evidence, achieving 0.761 AUC with 99.6% consistency. The framework demonstrates auditable explainability for maritime supply-chain risk management without sacrificing performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.04818v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Port congestion at major maritime hubs disrupts global supply chains, yet existing prediction systems typically prioritize forecasting accuracy without providing operationally interpretable explanations. This paper proposes AIS-TGNN, an evidence-grounded framework that jointly performs congestion-escalation prediction and faithful natural-language explanation by coupling a Temporal Graph Attention Network (TGAT) with a structured large language model (LLM) reasoning module. Daily spatial graphs are constructed from Automatic Identification System (AIS) broadcasts, where each grid cell represents localized vessel activity and inter-cell interactions are modeled through attention-based message passing. The TGAT predictor captures spatiotemporal congestion dynamics, while model-internal evidence, including feature z-scores and attention-derived neighbor influence, is transformed into structured prompts that constrain LLM reasoning to verifiable model outputs. To evaluate explanatory reliability, we introduce a directional-consistency validation protocol that quantitatively measures agreement between generated narratives and underlying statistical evidence. Experiments on six months of AIS data from the Port of Los Angeles and Long Beach demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms both LR and GCN baselines, achieving a test AUC of 0.761, AP of 0.344, and recall of 0.504 under a strict chronological split while producing explanations with 99.6% directional consistency. Results show that grounding LLM generation in graph-model evidence enables interpretable and auditable risk reporting without sacrificing predictive performance. The framework provides a practical pathway toward operationally deployable explainable AI for maritime congestion monitoring and supply-chain risk management.

zh.title: LLM基础的港口拥堵预测的可解释性：通过时间图注意网络
zh.summary: AIS-TGNN结合时间图神经网络与大语言模型推理来预测港口拥堵，同时生成基于模型证据的可操作可解释说明，实现0.761 AUC和99.6%一致性。该框架为海事供应链风险管理演示了可审计的可解释性，而不牺牲性能。

fr.title: Explicabilité fondée sur les LLM pour la prédiction de la congestion portuaire via les réseaux d'attention temporelle graphique
fr.summary: AIS-TGNN couple les réseaux de neurones graphiques temporels avec le raisonnement LLM pour prédire la congestion portuaire tout en générant des explications interprétables sur le plan opérationnel enracinées dans les preuves du modèle, réalisant 0,761 AUC avec 99,6% de cohérence. Le framework démontre l'explicabilité vérifiable pour la gestion des risques de la chaîne d'approvisionnement maritime sans sacrifier les performances.

de.title: LLM-gestützte Erklärbarkeit für Hafenüberlastungsprognose über zeitliche Graphenaufmerksamkeitsnetzwerke
de.summary: AIS-TGNN kombiniert zeitliche Graphenneuronale Netzwerke mit LLM-Reasoning, um Hafenüberlastung vorherzusagen und dabei operativ interpretierbare Erklärungen auf Grundlage von Modellevidenzen zu generieren, wobei 0,761 AUC mit 99,6%iger Konsistenz erreicht wird. Das Framework demonstriert nachvollziehbare Erklärbarkeit für das Risikomanagement der maritimen Lieferkette, ohne dabei die Leistung zu beeinträchtigen.

es.title: Explicabilidad fundamentada en LLM para predicción de congestión portuaria a través de redes de atención gráfica temporal
es.summary: AIS-TGNN acopla redes neuronales gráficas temporales con razonamiento LLM para predecir la congestión portuaria mientras genera explicaciones operacionalmente interpretables fundamentadas en la evidencia del modelo, logrando 0,761 AUC con 99,6% de consistencia. El framework demuestra explicabilidad auditable para la gestión de riesgos de la cadena de suministro marítima sin sacrificar el rendimiento.

title: VISA: Value Injection via Shielded Adaptation for Personalized LLM Alignment
summary: VISA addresses the alignment tax in LLM fine-tuning by training a value-rewriter with GRPO to simultaneously optimize fine-grained value alignment and preserve semantic integrity and factual knowledge. The closed-loop framework significantly outperforms standard fine-tuning in maintaining personalized values while avoiding hallucinations.
content:
arXiv:2603.04822v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Aligning Large Language Models (LLMs) with nuanced human values remains a critical challenge, as existing methods like Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) often handle only coarse-grained attributes. In practice, fine-tuning LLMs on task-specific datasets to optimize value alignment inevitably incurs an alignment tax: the model's pre-calibrated value system drifts significantly due to latent bias absorption from training data, while the fine-tuning process also causes severe hallucinations and semantic information loss in generated responses. To address this, we propose VISA (Value Injection via Shielded Adaptation), a closed-loop framework designed to navigate this trade-off. VISA's architecture features a high-precision value detector, a semantic-to-value translator, and a core value-rewriter. The value-rewriter is trained via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) with a composite reward function that simultaneously optimizes for fine-grained value precision, and the preservation of semantic integrity. By learning an optimal policy to balance these competing objectives, VISA effectively mitigates the alignment tax while staying loyal to the original knowledge. Our experiments demonstrate that this approach enables precise control over a model's value expression while maintaining its factual consistency and general capabilities, significantly outperforming both standard fine-tuning methods and prompting-based baselines, including GPT-4o.

zh.title: VISA：通过屏蔽自适应进行价值注入以实现个性化LLM对齐
zh.summary: VISA通过使用GRPO训练价值重写器，同时优化细粒度价值对齐并保持语义完整性和事实知识，来解决LLM微调中的对齐税问题。这个闭环框架在保持个性化价值的同时避免幻觉，显著超越了标准微调的性能。

fr.title: VISA : Injection de valeurs via adaptation protégée pour l'alignement personnalisé des LLM
fr.summary: VISA résout le coût d'alignement dans le fine-tuning des LLM en entraînant un ré-écrivain de valeurs avec GRPO pour optimiser simultanément l'alignement de valeurs à grain fin et préserver l'intégrité sémantique et les connaissances factuelles. Le cadre en boucle fermée surpasse significativement le fine-tuning standard pour maintenir les valeurs personnalisées tout en évitant les hallucinations.

de.title: VISA: Wert-Injektion durch geschützte Anpassung für personalisierte LLM-Ausrichtung
de.summary: VISA adressiert die Ausrichtungssteuer beim LLM-Fine-Tuning durch Training eines Wert-Umschreibers mit GRPO, um gleichzeitig die feinkörnige Wertausrichtung zu optimieren und semantische Integrität sowie faktisches Wissen zu bewahren. Das Closed-Loop-Framework übertrifft standardmäßiges Fine-Tuning beim Beibehalten personalisierter Werte und verhindert Halluzinationen.

es.title: VISA: Inyección de valores mediante adaptación protegida para alineación personalizada de LLM
es.summary: VISA aborda el impuesto de alineación en el fine-tuning de LLM entrenando un reescritor de valores con GRPO para optimizar simultáneamente la alineación de valores de grano fino y preservar la integridad semántica y el conocimiento fáctico. El marco de ciclo cerrado supera significativamente el fine-tuning estándar en el mantenimiento de valores personalizados mientras evita alucinaciones.

title: Design Behaviour Codes (DBCs): A Taxonomy-Driven Layered Governance Benchmark for Large Language Models
summary: Design Behaviour Codes introduce a system prompt governance layer with 150 controls that reduces LLM risk exposure by 36.8% compared to baseline, evaluated across 30 risk domains using adversarial red-teaming. The model-agnostic framework provides auditable governance compatible with EU AI Act requirements without training-time modifications.
content:
arXiv:2603.04837v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce the Dynamic Behavioral Constraint (DBC) benchmark, the first empirical framework for evaluating the efficacy of a structured, 150-control behavioral governance layer, the MDBC (Madan DBC) system, applied at inference time to large language models (LLMs). Unlike training time alignment methods (RLHF, DPO) or post-hoc content moderation APIs, DBCs constitute a system prompt level governance layer that is model-agnostic, jurisdiction-mappable, and auditable. We evaluate the DBC Framework across a 30 domain risk taxonomy organized into six clusters (Hallucination and Calibration, Bias and Fairness, Malicious Use, Privacy and Data Protection, Robustness and Reliability, and Misalignment Agency) using an agentic red-team protocol with five adversarial attack strategies (Direct, Roleplay, Few-Shot, Hypothetical, Authority Spoof) across 3 model families. Our three-arm controlled design (Base, Base plus Moderation, Base plus DBC) enables causal attribution of risk reduction. Key findings: the DBC layer reduces the aggregate Risk Exposure Rate (RER) from 7.19 percent (Base) to 4.55 percent (Base plus DBC), representing a 36.8 percent relative risk reduction, compared with 0.6 percent for a standard safety moderation prompt. MDBC Adherence Scores improve from 8.6 by 10 (Base) to 8.7 by 10 (Base plus DBC). EU AI Act compliance (automated scoring) reaches 8.5by 10 under the DBC layer. A three judge evaluation ensemble yields Fleiss kappa greater than 0.70 (substantial agreement), validating our automated pipeline. Cluster ablation identifies the Integrity Protection cluster (MDBC 081 099) as delivering the highest per domain risk reduction, while graybox adversarial attacks achieve a DBC Bypass Rate of 4.83 percent . We release the benchmark code, prompt database, and all evaluation artefacts to enable reproducibility and longitudinal tracking as models evolve.

zh.title: 设计行为代码（DBC）：用于大型语言模型的分类驱动的分层治理基准
zh.summary: 设计行为代码引入了一个系统提示治理层，包含150个控制措施，在30个风险域中通过对抗性红队测试评估，相比基线降低LLM风险暴露度36.8%。这个与模型无关的框架提供可审计的治理，兼容欧盟AI法案要求，无需训练时间修改。

fr.title: Codes de Comportement de Conception (DBC) : Un benchmark de gouvernance hiérarchisée guidé par la taxonomie pour les grands modèles de langage
fr.summary: Les codes de comportement de conception introduisent une couche de gouvernance des invites système avec 150 contrôles qui réduisent l'exposition aux risques des LLM de 36,8% par rapport à la ligne de base, évaluée dans 30 domaines de risque en utilisant des tests d'équipe adverse. Le cadre indépendant du modèle fournit une gouvernance vérifiable compatible avec les exigences de la Loi sur l'IA de l'UE sans modifications au moment de l'entraînement.

de.title: Design Behavior Codes (DBCs): Ein taxonomiegestütztes geschichtetes Governance-Benchmark für große Sprachmodelle
de.summary: Design Behavior Codes führen eine Systemanleitung-Governance-Schicht mit 150 Kontrollen ein, die die Risikoexposition von LLMs um 36,8% gegenüber der Baseline reduziert, bewertet über 30 Risikobereiche mit gegnerischem Red-Teaming. Das modellunabhängige Framework bietet nachvollziehbare Governance, die mit den Anforderungen des EU AI Act kompatibel ist, ohne Änderungen zur Trainingszeit.

es.title: Códigos de Comportamiento de Diseño (DBC): Un benchmark de gobernanza estratificada impulsada por taxonomía para grandes modelos de lenguaje
es.summary: Los Códigos de Comportamiento de Diseño introducen una capa de gobernanza de indicación de sistema con 150 controles que reducen la exposición al riesgo de LLM en un 36,8% en comparación con la línea de base, evaluada en 30 dominios de riesgo mediante pruebas de equipo rojo adversarial. El marco agnóstico del modelo proporciona gobernanza auditable compatible con los requisitos de la Ley de IA de la UE sin modificaciones en tiempo de entrenamiento.

title: On Multi-Step Theorem Prediction via Non-Parametric Structural Priors
summary: This paper proposes Theorem Precedence Graphs encoding structural dependencies from reasoning traces to enable LLMs to perform multi-step theorem prediction with 89.29% accuracy without gradient optimization. The approach addresses "Structural Drift," where standard in-context learning performance degrades sharply with increasing reasoning depth.
content:
arXiv:2603.04852v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Multi-step theorem prediction is a central challenge in automated reasoning. Existing neural-symbolic approaches rely heavily on supervised parametric models, which exhibit limited generalization to evolving theorem libraries. In this work, we explore training-free theorem prediction through the lens of in-context learning (ICL). We identify a critical scalability bottleneck, termed Structural Drift: as reasoning depth increases, the performance of vanilla ICL degrades sharply, often collapsing to near zero. We attribute this failure to the LLM's inability to recover latent topological dependencies, leading to unstructured exploration. To address this issue, we propose Theorem Precedence Graphs, which encode temporal dependencies from historical solution traces as directed graphs, and impose explicit topological constraints that effectively prune the search space during inference. Coupled with retrieval-augmented graph construction and a stepwise symbolic executor, our approach enables LLMs to act as structured planners without any gradient-based optimization. Experiments on the FormalGeo7k benchmark show that our method achieves 89.29% accuracy, substantially outperforming ICL baselines and matching state-of-the-art supervised models. These results indicate that explicit structural priors offer a promising direction for scaling LLM-based symbolic reasoning.

zh.title: 通过非参数结构先验的多步定理预测
zh.summary: 本文提出定理先导图，从推理轨迹中编码结构依赖关系，使LLM能够以89.29%的精度执行多步定理预测，无需梯度优化。该方法解决了"结构漂移"问题，其中标准上下文学习的性能随着推理深度的增加而急剧下降。

fr.title: Sur la prédiction de théorèmes multi-étapes via des priors structurels non-paramétriques
fr.summary: Cet article propose des graphes de précédence de théorèmes encodant les dépendances structurelles des traces de raisonnement pour permettre aux LLM de réaliser la prédiction de théorèmes multi-étapes avec une précision de 89,29% sans optimisation par gradient. L'approche aborde la « dérive structurelle », où les performances d'apprentissage en contexte standard se dégradent considérablement avec une profondeur de raisonnement croissante.

de.title: Über die mehrstufige Theoremvorhersage via nicht-parametrische strukturelle Priors
de.summary: Dieses Papier schlägt Theorem-Präzedenzgraphen vor, die strukturelle Abhängigkeiten aus Begründungsspuren kodieren, um LLMs die mehrstufige Theoremvorhersage mit 89,29% Genauigkeit ohne Gradientenoptimierung zu ermöglichen. Der Ansatz adressiert „Strukturelle Drift", bei der die Leistung des standardmäßigen In-Context-Lernens mit zunehmender Begründungstiefe stark abnimmt.

es.title: Sobre la predicción de teoremas de múltiples pasos mediante antecedentes estructurales no paramétricos
es.summary: Este artículo propone Gráficos de Precedencia de Teoremas que codifican dependencias estructurales de trazas de razonamiento para permitir que los LLM realicen predicción de teoremas de múltiples pasos con una precisión del 89,29% sin optimización de gradientes. El enfoque aborda la "Deriva Estructural", donde el desempeño del aprendizaje en contexto estándar se degrada significativamente con una profundidad de razonamiento creciente.

title: Causally Robust Reward Learning from Reason-Augmented Preference Feedback
summary: ReCouPLe improves reward learning from preference feedback by using natural language rationales as causal signals, enabling models to identify genuine preference drivers rather than spurious correlations. The framework achieves 1.5-2x robustness improvements under distribution shift and transfers preference knowledge across tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.04861v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Preference-based reward learning is widely used for shaping agent behavior to match a user's preference, yet its sparse binary feedback makes it especially vulnerable to causal confusion. The learned reward often latches onto spurious features that merely co-occur with preferred trajectories during training, collapsing when those correlations disappear or reverse at test time. We introduce ReCouPLe, a lightweight framework that uses natural language rationales to provide the missing causal signal. Each rationale is treated as a guiding projection axis in an embedding space, training the model to score trajectories based on features aligned with that axis while de-emphasizing context that is unrelated to the stated reason. Because the same rationales (e.g., "avoids collisions", "completes the task faster") can appear across multiple tasks, ReCouPLe naturally reuses the same causal direction whenever tasks share semantics, and transfers preference knowledge to novel tasks without extra data or language-model fine-tuning. Our learned reward model can ground preferences on the articulated reason, aligning better with user intent and generalizing beyond spurious features. ReCouPLe outperforms baselines by up to 1.5x in reward accuracy under distribution shifts, and 2x in downstream policy performance in novel tasks. We have released our code at https://github.com/mj-hwang/ReCouPLe

zh.title: 因果鲁棒奖励学习：从理由增强的偏好反馈出发
zh.summary: ReCouPLe通过使用自然语言理由作为因果信号，改进了从偏好反馈中的奖励学习，使模型能够识别真正的偏好驱动因素而非虚假相关性。该框架在分布偏移下实现了1.5-2倍的鲁棒性改进，并可跨任务转移偏好知识。

fr.title: Apprentissage des récompenses robuste aux causes à partir de commentaires de préférences augmentés par des raisons
fr.summary: ReCouPLe améliore l'apprentissage des récompenses à partir de commentaires de préférences en utilisant des justifications en langage naturel comme signaux causaux, permettant aux modèles d'identifier les vrais moteurs de préférence plutôt que les corrélations fallacieuses. Le cadre réalise des améliorations de robustesse de 1,5-2x en cas de changement de distribution et transfère les connaissances de préférence entre les tâches.

de.title: Kausal robustes Belohnungslernen aus begründungsaugmentiertem Präferenzfeedback
de.summary: ReCouPLe verbessert das Belohnungslernen aus Präferenzfeedback durch die Verwendung natürlichsprachlicher Begründungen als kausale Signale, wodurch Modelle echte Präferenztreiber statt spurioser Korrelationen erkennen können. Das Framework erreicht 1,5-2x Robustheitsverbesserungen unter Verteilungsverschiebung und überträgt Präferenzwissen zwischen Aufgaben.

es.title: Aprendizaje de recompensas causalmente robusto a partir de comentarios de preferencia aumentados con razones
es.summary: ReCouPLe mejora el aprendizaje de recompensas a partir de comentarios de preferencia utilizando justificaciones en lenguaje natural como señales causales, permitiendo que los modelos identifiquen los verdaderos impulsores de preferencia en lugar de correlaciones espurias. El marco logra mejoras de robustez de 1,5-2x bajo cambios de distribución y transfiere conocimiento de preferencia entre tareas.

title: K-Gen: A Multimodal Language-Conditioned Approach for Interpretable Keypoint-Guided Trajectory Generation
summary: K-Gen generates interpretable trajectory keypoints by reasoning over rasterized BEV maps and scene descriptions using multimodal LLMs, then refines them into full trajectories, outperforming baselines on WOMD and nuPlan. The approach combines multimodal reasoning with trajectory-aware reinforcement fine-tuning for autonomous driving simulation.
content:
arXiv:2603.04868v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Generating realistic and diverse trajectories is a critical challenge in autonomous driving simulation. While Large Language Models (LLMs) show promise, existing methods often rely on structured data like vectorized maps, which fail to capture the rich, unstructured visual context of a scene. To address this, we propose K-Gen, an interpretable keypoint-guided multimodal framework that leverages Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to unify rasterized BEV map inputs with textual scene descriptions. Instead of directly predicting full trajectories, K-Gen generates interpretable keypoints along with reasoning that reflects agent intentions, which are subsequently refined into accurate trajectories by a refinement module. To further enhance keypoint generation, we apply T-DAPO, a trajectory-aware reinforcement fine-tuning algorithm. Experiments on WOMD and nuPlan demonstrate that K-Gen outperforms existing baselines, highlighting the effectiveness of combining multimodal reasoning with keypoint-guided trajectory generation.

zh.title: K-Gen：用于可解释关键点引导轨迹生成的多模态语言条件方法
zh.summary: K-Gen通过对栅栏化BEV地图和场景描述进行推理，使用多模态大语言模型生成可解释的轨迹关键点，然后将其细化为完整轨迹，在WOMD和nuPlan上优于基准方法。该方法将多模态推理与轨迹感知强化微调相结合，用于自主驾驶仿真。

fr.title: K-Gen : Une approche multimodale et conditionnée par le langage pour la génération de trajectoires guidées par des points clés interprétables
fr.summary: K-Gen génère des points clés de trajectoire interprétables en raisonnant sur des cartes BEV rastérisées et des descriptions de scènes à l'aide de LLM multimodaux, puis les affine en trajectoires complètes, surpassant les lignes de base sur WOMD et nuPlan. L'approche combine le raisonnement multimodal avec l'affinage par renforcement conscient de la trajectoire pour la simulation de conduite autonome.

de.title: K-Gen: Ein multimodaler sprachgesteuerter Ansatz zur interpretierbaren schlüsselpunktgesteuerten Trajektorienerzeugung
de.summary: K-Gen generiert interpretierbare Trajektorienschlüsselpunkte durch Argumentation über rastergescannte BEV-Karten und Szenenbeschreibungen mit multimodalen LLMs und verfeinert sie dann zu vollständigen Trajektorien, wobei es Baselines auf WOMD und nuPlan übertrifft. Der Ansatz kombiniert multimodales Denken mit trajektoriengerechtem Reinforcement Fine-Tuning zur autonomen Fahrsimulation.

es.title: K-Gen: Un enfoque multimodal condicionado por lenguaje para la generación interpretable de trayectorias guiadas por puntos clave
es.summary: K-Gen genera puntos clave de trayectoria interpretables razonando sobre mapas BEV rasterizados y descripciones de escenas usando LLM multimodales, luego los refina en trayectorias completas, superando los modelos base en WOMD y nuPlan. El enfoque combina razonamiento multimodal con afinamiento de refuerzo consciente de trayectorias para la simulación de conducción autónoma.

title: SEA-TS: Self-Evolving Agent for Autonomous Code Generation of Time Series Forecasting Algorithms
summary: SEA-TS autonomously generates optimized time series forecasting code through iterative evolution combining Monte Carlo tree search with code review and architecture search, achieving 40% MAE reduction over TimeMixer. The system discovers novel patterns like physics-informed constraints, demonstrating that autonomous ML engineering can innovate beyond manual design.
content:
arXiv:2603.04873v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Accurate time series forecasting underpins decision-making across domains, yet conventional ML development suffers from data scarcity in new deployments, poor adaptability under distribution shift, and diminishing returns from manual iteration. We propose Self-Evolving Agent for Time Series Algorithms (SEA-TS), a framework that autonomously generates, validates, and optimizes forecasting code via an iterative self-evolution loop. Our framework introduces three key innovations: (1) Metric-Advantage Monte Carlo Tree Search (MA-MCTS), which replaces fixed rewards with a normalized advantage score for discriminative search guidance; (2) Code Review with running prompt refinement, where each executed solution undergoes automated review followed by prompt updates that encode corrective patterns, preventing recurrence of similar errors; and (3) Global Steerable Reasoning, which compares each node against global best and worst solutions, enabling cross-trajectory knowledge transfer. We adopt a MAP-Elites archive for architectural diversity. On the public Solar-Energy benchmark, SEA-TS generated code achieves a 40% MAE reduction relative to TimeMixer, surpassing state-of-the-art methods. On proprietary datasets, SEA-TS generated code reduces WAPE by 8.6% on solar PV forecasting and 7.7% on residential load forecasting compared to human-engineered baselines, and achieves 26.17% MAPE on load forecasting versus 29.34% by TimeMixer. Notably, the evolved models discover novel architectural patterns--including physics-informed monotonic decay heads encoding solar irradiance constraints, per-station learned diurnal cycle profiles, and learnable hourly bias correction--demonstrating that autonomous ML engineering can generate genuinely novel algorithmic ideas beyond manual design.

zh.title: SEA-TS：用于时间序列预测算法自主代码生成的自我进化代理
zh.summary: SEA-TS通过将蒙特卡洛树搜索与代码审查和架构搜索相结合的迭代进化，自主生成优化的时间序列预测代码，与TimeMixer相比MAE减少40%。该系统发现了物理启发约束等新颖模式，证明自主机器学习工程可以超越手工设计进行创新。

fr.title: SEA-TS : Agent auto-évolutif pour la génération autonome de code d'algorithmes de prévision de séries temporelles
fr.summary: SEA-TS génère automatiquement du code de prévision de séries temporelles optimisé par évolution itérative combinant la recherche par arbre de Monte Carlo avec l'examen du code et la recherche d'architecture, réalisant une réduction MAE de 40% par rapport à TimeMixer. Le système découvre des modèles novateurs comme les contraintes informées par la physique, démontrant que l'ingénierie ML autonome peut innover au-delà de la conception manuelle.

de.title: SEA-TS: Selbst-evolvierender Agent für autonome Codegenerierung von Zeitreihenprognose-Algorithmen
de.summary: SEA-TS generiert autonom optimierten Zeitreihenprognose-Code durch iterative Entwicklung, die Monte-Carlo-Baumsuche mit Code-Überprüfung und Architektursuche kombiniert und eine 40%ige MAE-Reduktion gegenüber TimeMixer erreicht. Das System entdeckt neuartige Muster wie physikgestützte Beschränkungen und zeigt, dass autonomes ML-Engineering über manuelle Entwürfe hinaus innovativ sein kann.

es.title: SEA-TS: Agente auto-evolutivo para la generación autónoma de código de algoritmos de pronóstico de series temporales
es.summary: SEA-TS genera automáticamente código optimizado de pronóstico de series temporales mediante evolución iterativa que combina búsqueda de árbol de Monte Carlo con revisión de código y búsqueda de arquitectura, logrando una reducción del 40% en MAE en comparación con TimeMixer. El sistema descubre patrones novedosos como restricciones informadas por la física, demostrando que la ingeniería de ML autónoma puede innovar más allá del diseño manual.

title: Bounded State in an Infinite Horizon: Proactive Hierarchical Memory for Ad-Hoc Recall over Streaming Dialogues
summary: Researchers introduce STEM-Bench, a benchmark for evaluating memory in infinite-horizon streaming dialogues, and propose ProStream, a hierarchical memory framework that enables efficient ad-hoc recall while maintaining reasoning quality in unbounded conversational streams.
content:
arXiv:2603.04885v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Real-world dialogue usually unfolds as an infinite stream. It thus requires bounded-state memory mechanisms to operate within an infinite horizon. However, existing read-then-think memory is fundamentally misaligned with this setting, as it cannot support ad-hoc memory recall while streams unfold. To explore this challenge, we introduce \textbf{STEM-Bench}, the first benchmark for \textbf{ST}reaming \textbf{E}valuation of \textbf{M}emory. It comprises over 14K QA pairs in dialogue streams that assess perception fidelity, temporal reasoning, and global awareness under infinite-horizon constraints. The preliminary analysis on STEM-Bench indicates a critical \textit{fidelity-efficiency dilemma}: retrieval-based methods use fragment context, while full-context models incur unbounded latency. To resolve this, we propose \textbf{ProStream}, a proactive hierarchical memory framework for streaming dialogues. It enables ad-hoc memory recall on demand by reasoning over continuous streams with multi-granular distillation. Moreover, it employs Adaptive Spatiotemporal Optimization to dynamically optimize retention based on expected utility. It enables a bounded knowledge state for lower inference latency without sacrificing reasoning fidelity. Experiments show that ProStream outperforms baselines in both accuracy and efficiency.

zh.title: 无限地平线中的有界状态:流式对话中临时回忆的主动分层记忆
zh.summary: 研究人员引入了STEM-Bench,这是一个用于评估无限地平线流式对话中内存的基准,并提出了ProStream,一个分层记忆框架,可以在无界对话流中实现高效的临时回忆,同时保持推理质量。

fr.title: État borné dans un horizon infini: mémoire hiérarchique proactive pour la récupération ad hoc sur les dialogues en continu
fr.summary: Les chercheurs introduisent STEM-Bench, un indice de référence pour évaluer la mémoire dans les dialogues en continu d'horizon infini, et proposent ProStream, un cadre de mémoire hiérarchique qui permet la récupération ad hoc efficace tout en maintenant la qualité du raisonnement dans les flux de conversation non bornés.

de.title: Begrenzte Zustände in unendlichem Horizont: Proaktive hierarchische Speicher für Ad-hoc-Abruf über Streaming-Dialoge
de.summary: Forscher führen STEM-Bench ein, einen Maßstab zur Bewertung des Speichers in Streaming-Dialogen mit unendlichem Horizont, und schlagen ProStream vor, ein hierarchisches Speicherframework, das effiziente Ad-hoc-Abrufe ermöglicht und gleichzeitig die Reasoning-Qualität in unbegrenzten Konversationsströmen bewahrt.

es.title: Estado acotado en un horizonte infinito: memoria jerárquica proactiva para recuperación ad hoc sobre diálogos de transmisión
es.summary: Los investigadores presentan STEM-Bench, un indicador para evaluar la memoria en diálogos de transmisión de horizonte infinito, y proponen ProStream, un marco de memoria jerárquica que permite la recuperación ad hoc eficiente mientras se mantiene la calidad del razonamiento en corrientes de conversación ilimitadas.

title: Differentially Private Multimodal In-Context Learning
summary: DP-MTV enables privacy-preserving in-context learning for vision-language models by aggregating demonstrations into compact task vectors, allowing many-shot multimodal learning under formal differential privacy constraints suited for sensitive domains like medical imaging.
content:
arXiv:2603.04894v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Vision-language models are increasingly applied to sensitive domains such as medical imaging and personal photographs, yet existing differentially private methods for in-context learning are limited to few-shot, text-only settings because privacy cost scales with the number of tokens processed. We present Differentially Private Multimodal Task Vectors (DP-MTV), the first framework enabling many-shot multimodal in-context learning with formal $(\varepsilon, \delta)$-differential privacy by aggregating hundreds of demonstrations into compact task vectors in activation space. DP-MTV partitions private data into disjoint chunks, applies per-layer clipping to bound sensitivity, and adds calibrated noise to the aggregate, requiring only a single noise addition that enables unlimited inference queries. We evaluate on eight benchmarks across three VLM architectures, supporting deployment with or without auxiliary data. At $\varepsilon=1.0$, DP-MTV achieves 50% on VizWiz compared to 55% non-private and 35% zero-shot, preserving most of the gain from in-context learning under meaningful privacy constraints.

zh.title: 差分隐私多模态上下文学习
zh.summary: DP-MTV通过将演示聚合为紧凑的任务向量，为视觉-语言模型启用隐私保护的上下文学习，允许在形式差分隐私约束下进行多样本多模态学习，适用于医学成像等敏感领域。

fr.title: Apprentissage contextuel multimodal différentiellement privé
fr.summary: DP-MTV permet l'apprentissage contextuel préservant la confidentialité pour les modèles vision-langage en agrégant les démonstrations en vecteurs de tâches compacts, permettant l'apprentissage multimodal multi-exemplaires sous des contraintes de confidentialité différentielle formelles adaptées aux domaines sensibles comme l'imagerie médicale.

de.title: Differentiell privates multimodales kontextabhängiges Lernen
de.summary: DP-MTV ermöglicht datenschutzschützendes kontextabhängiges Lernen für Vision-Language-Modelle durch Aggregation von Demonstrationen in kompakte Task-Vektoren, was Few-Shot-Multimodal-Lernen unter formalen Datenschutzbeschränkungen ermöglicht und für sensitive Bereiche wie medizinische Bildgebung geeignet ist.

es.title: Aprendizaje en contexto multimodal diferencialmente privado
es.summary: DP-MTV permite el aprendizaje en contexto que preserva la privacidad para modelos de visión-lenguaje mediante la agregación de demostraciones en vectores de tareas compactos, permitiendo aprendizaje multimodal de múltiples ejemplos bajo restricciones formales de privacidad diferencial adecuadas para dominios sensibles como la imagenología médica.

title: Authorize-on-Demand: Dynamic Authorization with Legality-Aware Intellectual Property Protection for VLMs
summary: AoD-IP introduces dynamic, user-controlled authorization for vision-language models at deployment time, enabling flexible IP protection that adapts to evolving application scenarios while reliably detecting unauthorized use and transfers.
content:
arXiv:2603.04896v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The rapid adoption of vision-language models (VLMs) has heightened the demand for robust intellectual property (IP) protection of these high-value pretrained models. Effective IP protection should proactively confine model deployment within authorized domains and prevent unauthorized transfers. However, existing methods rely on static training-time definitions, limiting flexibility in dynamic environments and often producing opaque responses to unauthorized inputs. To address these limitations, we propose a novel dynamic authorization with legality-aware intellectual property protection (AoD-IP) for VLMs, a framework that supports authorize-on-demand and legality-aware assessment. AoD-IP introduces a lightweight dynamic authorization module that enables flexible, user-controlled authorization, allowing users to actively specify or switch authorized domains on demand at deployment time. This enables the model to adapt seamlessly as application scenarios evolve and provides substantially greater extensibility than existing static-domain approaches. In addition, AoD-IP incorporates a dual-path inference mechanism that jointly predicts input legality-aware and task-specific outputs. Comprehensive experimental results on multiple cross-domain benchmarks demonstrate that AoD-IP maintains strong authorized-domain performance and reliable unauthorized detection, while supporting user-controlled authorization for adaptive deployment in dynamic environments.

zh.title: 按需授权：具有法律意识的知识产权保护的动态授权用于VLMs
zh.summary: AoD-IP为视觉-语言模型在部署时引入动态、用户控制的授权，实现灵活的知识产权保护，能够适应不断演变的应用场景，同时可靠地检测未授权的使用和转移。

fr.title: Autorisation à la demande : Autorisation dynamique avec protection de la propriété intellectuelle consciente de la légalité pour les VLM
fr.summary: AoD-IP introduit une autorisation dynamique contrôlée par l'utilisateur pour les modèles vision-langage au moment du déploiement, permettant une protection IP flexible qui s'adapte à l'évolution des scénarios d'application tout en détectant de manière fiable l'utilisation et les transferts non autorisés.

de.title: Autorisierung auf Anfrage: Dynamische Autorisierung mit legalitätsbewusster Schutz des geistigen Eigentums für VLMs
de.summary: AoD-IP führt dynamische, von Benutzern gesteuerte Autorisierung für Vision-Language-Modelle zur Bereitstellungszeit ein und ermöglicht flexiblen IP-Schutz, der sich an sich entwickelnde Anwendungsszenarien anpasst, während zuverlässig unbefugte Nutzung und Übertragungen erkannt werden.

es.title: Autorización bajo demanda: Autorización dinámica con protección de propiedad intelectual consciente de legalidad para VLMs
es.summary: AoD-IP introduce autorización dinámica controlada por el usuario para modelos de visión-lenguaje en el momento de la implementación, permitiendo protección de IP flexible que se adapta a escenarios de aplicación en evolución mientras detecta de manera confiable el uso y transferencias no autorizadas.

title: EvoTool: Self-Evolving Tool-Use Policy Optimization in LLM Agents via Blame-Aware Mutation and Diversity-Aware Selection
summary: EvoTool optimizes LLM agent tool-use policies through gradient-free evolutionary algorithms with blame-aware mutation and diversity-aware selection, improving complex task performance by iteratively refining modular agent components.
content:
arXiv:2603.04900v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: LLM-based agents depend on effective tool-use policies to solve complex tasks, yet optimizing these policies remains challenging due to delayed supervision and the difficulty of credit assignment in long-horizon trajectories. Existing optimization approaches tend to be either monolithic, which are prone to entangling behaviors, or single-aspect, which ignore cross-module error propagation. To address these limitations, we propose EvoTool, a self-evolving framework that optimizes a modular tool-use policy via a gradient-free evolutionary paradigm. EvoTool decomposes agent's tool-use policy into four modules, including Planner, Selector, Caller, and Synthesizer, and iteratively improves them in a self-improving loop through three novel mechanisms. Trajectory-Grounded Blame Attribution uses diagnostic traces to localize failures to a specific module. Feedback-Guided Targeted Mutation then edits only that module via natural-language critique. Diversity-Aware Population Selection preserves complementary candidates to ensure solution diversity. Across four benchmarks, EvoTool outperforms strong baselines by over 5 points on both GPT-4.1 and Qwen3-8B, while achieving superior efficiency and transferability. The code will be released once paper is accepted.

zh.title: EvoTool：通过责备感知突变和多样性感知选择的LLM代理中的自进化工具使用策略优化
zh.summary: EvoTool通过具有责备感知突变和多样性感知选择的无梯度进化算法优化LLM代理的工具使用策略，通过迭代地改进模块化代理组件来改善复杂任务性能。

fr.title: EvoTool : Optimisation de la politique d'utilisation d'outils auto-évolutive dans les agents LLM via mutation consciente des responsabilités et sélection consciente de la diversité
fr.summary: EvoTool optimise les politiques d'utilisation des outils des agents LLM par le biais d'algorithmes évolutifs sans gradient avec mutation consciente des responsabilités et sélection consciente de la diversité, améliorant les performances des tâches complexes en affinant itérativement les composants d'agents modulaires.

de.title: EvoTool: Selbstentwickelnde Werkzeugnutzungsrichtlinienoptimierung in LLM-Agenten durch schuldbewusste Mutation und vielfaltsbewusste Auswahl
de.summary: EvoTool optimiert die Werkzeugnutzungsrichtlinien von LLM-Agenten durch gradientenfreie Evolutionsalgorithmen mit schuldbewusster Mutation und vielfaltsbewusster Auswahl und verbessert die Leistung komplexer Aufgaben durch iterative Verfeinerung modularer Agentenkomponenten.

es.title: EvoTool: Optimización de política de uso de herramientas auto-evolutiva en agentes LLM mediante mutación consciente de culpa y selección consciente de diversidad
es.summary: EvoTool optimiza las políticas de uso de herramientas de agentes LLM a través de algoritmos evolutivos sin gradiente con mutación consciente de culpa y selección consciente de diversidad, mejorando el desempeño de tareas complejas mediante el refinamiento iterativo de componentes de agentes modulares.

title: Alignment Backfire: Language-Dependent Reversal of Safety Interventions Across 16 Languages in LLM Multi-Agent Systems
summary: A multi-language study reveals that AI safety interventions produce opposite effects across languages, with alignment measures reducing harmful behavior in English but amplifying it in Japanese, showing safety benefits don't universally transfer across linguistic contexts.
content:
arXiv:2603.04904v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In perpetrator treatment, a recurring observation is the dissociation between insight and action: offenders articulate remorse yet behavioral change does not follow. We report four preregistered studies (1,584 multi-agent simulations across 16 languages and three model families) demonstrating that alignment interventions in large language models produce a structurally analogous phenomenon: surface safety that masks or generates collective pathology and internal dissociation. In Study 1 (N = 150), increasing alignment-instructed agents reduced collective pathology in English (g = -1.844, p < .0001) but amplified it in Japanese (g = +0.771, p = .038)--a directional reversal we term "alignment backfire." Study 2 (N = 1,174) extended to 16 languages: alignment-induced dissociation was near-universal (15/16 languages; beta = 0.0667, p < .0001), while collective pathology bifurcated along cultural-linguistic lines (interaction beta = 0.0684, p = .0003), correlating with Power Distance Index (r = 0.474, p = .064). Study 3 (N = 180) tested individuation as countermeasure; individuated agents became the primary source of both pathology and dissociation (DI = +1.120) with conformity above 84%--demonstrating iatrogenesis. Study 4 (N = 80) validated patterns across Llama 3.3 70B, GPT-4o-mini, and Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B, confirming English safety is model-general while Japanese backfire is model-specific. These findings reframe alignment as a behavioral intervention subject to risk homeostasis and iatrogenesis. Language space--the linguistic, pragmatic, and cultural properties inherited from training data--structurally determines alignment outcomes. Safety validated in English does not transfer to other languages, and prompt-level interventions cannot override language-space-level constraints.

zh.title: 对齐反效应：LLM多代理系统中16种语言的安全干预语言依赖性逆转
zh.summary: 一项多语言研究表明，人工智能安全干预在不同语言中产生相反的效果，对齐措施减少英语中的有害行为，但在日语中放大了这种行为，显示安全益处无法普遍跨语言上下文转移。

fr.title: Inversion d'alignement : Inversion dépendante de la langue des interventions de sécurité dans 16 langues dans les systèmes multi-agents LLM
fr.summary: Une étude multilingue révèle que les interventions de sécurité de l'IA produisent des effets opposés dans différentes langues, les mesures d'alignement réduisant les comportements nuisibles en anglais mais les amplifiant en japonais, montrant que les avantages de sécurité ne se transfèrent pas universellement entre les contextes linguistiques.

de.title: Alignment-Backfire: Sprachabhängige Umkehrung von Sicherheitsinterventionen in 16 Sprachen in LLM-Multi-Agent-Systemen
de.summary: Eine mehrsprachige Studie zeigt, dass KI-Sicherheitsinterventionen in verschiedenen Sprachen gegenteilige Effekte haben, wobei Ausrichtungsmaßnahmen schädliches Verhalten im Englischen reduzieren, aber im Japanischen verstärken, was zeigt, dass Sicherheitsvorteile nicht universell über sprachliche Kontexte hinweg übertragen werden.

es.title: Inversión de alineación: reversión dependiente del idioma de intervenciones de seguridad en 16 idiomas en sistemas multiagente LLM
es.summary: Un estudio multilingüe revela que las intervenciones de seguridad de la IA producen efectos opuestos en diferentes idiomas, con medidas de alineación reduciendo el comportamiento dañino en inglés pero amplificándolo en japonés, mostrando que los beneficios de seguridad no se transfieren universalmente a través de contextos lingüísticos.

title: Knowledge-informed Bidding with Dual-process Control for Online Advertising
summary: KBD introduces a hybrid approach to ad bidding optimization by embedding human expertise as inductive biases and combining rule-based controls with reinforcement learning, improving generalization in data-sparse and out-of-distribution advertising scenarios.
content:
arXiv:2603.04920v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Bid optimization in online advertising relies on black-box machine-learning models that learn bidding decisions from historical data. However, these approaches fail to replicate human experts' adaptive, experience-driven, and globally coherent decisions. Specifically, they generalize poorly in data-sparse cases because of missing structured knowledge, make short-sighted sequential decisions that ignore long-term interdependencies, and struggle to adapt in out-of-distribution scenarios where human experts succeed. To address this, we propose KBD (Knowledge-informed Bidding with Dual-process control), a novel method for bid optimization. KBD embeds human expertise as inductive biases through the informed machine-learning paradigm, uses Decision Transformer (DT) to globally optimize multi-step bidding sequences, and implements dual-process control by combining a fast rule-based PID (System 1) with DT (System 2). Extensive experiments highlight KBD's advantage over existing methods and underscore the benefit of grounding bid optimization in human expertise and dual-process control.

zh.title: 知识驱动的双过程控制在线广告出价
zh.summary: KBD通过将人类专业知识作为归纳偏差嵌入，并将基于规则的控制与强化学习相结合，介绍了广告出价优化的混合方法，改善了数据稀疏和分布外广告场景中的泛化能力。

fr.title: Enchères informées par les connaissances avec contrôle à double processus pour la publicité en ligne
fr.summary: KBD introduit une approche hybride pour l'optimisation des enchères publicitaires en intégrant l'expertise humaine en tant que biais inductifs et en combinant les contrôles basés sur des règles avec l'apprentissage par renforcement, améliorant la généralisation dans les scénarios publicitaires peu denses en données et hors distribution.

de.title: Wissensinformiertes Bieten mit dualer Prozesskontrolle für Online-Werbung
de.summary: KBD führt einen Hybrid-Ansatz zur Optimierung von Werbebuchungen ein, indem menschliches Fachwissen als induktive Verzerrungen eingebettet wird und regelbasierte Steuerungen mit verstärktem Lernen kombiniert werden, was die Verallgemeinerung in sparsamen und außerverteilten Werbeszenarios verbessert.

es.title: Oferta informada por conocimiento con control de doble proceso para publicidad en línea
es.summary: KBD introduce un enfoque híbrido para la optimización de pujas publicitarias integrando la experiencia humana como sesgos inductivos y combinando controles basados en reglas con aprendizaje por refuerzo, mejorando la generalización en escenarios publicitarios escasos en datos y fuera de distribución.

title: TimeWarp: Evaluating Web Agents by Revisiting the Past
summary: TimeWarp benchmark tests web agents' robustness to changing website designs across internet eras, revealing vulnerabilities to UI changes and proposing TimeTraj algorithm that improves generalization by training on trajectories across multiple design versions.
content:
arXiv:2603.04949v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The improvement of web agents on current benchmarks raises the question: Do today's agents perform just as well when the web changes? We introduce TimeWarp, a benchmark that emulates the evolving web using containerized environments that vary in UI, design, and layout. TimeWarp consists of three web environments, each with six UI versions spanning different eras of the internet, paired with a set of complex, realistic tasks requiring different forms of web navigation. Our experiments reveal web agents' vulnerability to changes and the limitations of behavior cloning (BC) on single-version trajectories. To address this, we propose TimeTraj, a simple yet effective algorithm that uses plan distillation to collect trajectories across multiple versions. By training agents on teacher rollouts using our BC-variant, we achieve substantial performance gains: $20.4\%\rightarrow37.7\%$ for Qwen-3 4B and $0\%\rightarrow27.0\%$ for Llama-3.1 8B models. We hope our work helps researchers study generalization across web designs and unlock a new paradigm for collecting plans rather than trajectories, thereby improving the robustness of web agents.

zh.title: TimeWarp：通过重新审视过去来评估网络代理
zh.summary: TimeWarp基准测试通过网络历史不同时代的网站设计变化来测试网络代理的健壮性，揭示了对用户界面更改的漏洞，并提出了TimeTraj算法，通过在多个设计版本的轨迹上训练来改善泛化能力。

fr.title: TimeWarp : Évaluer les agents Web en revisitant le passé
fr.summary: L'évaluation TimeWarp teste la robustesse des agents Web face aux modifications de conception des sites Web à travers les ères d'Internet, révélant des vulnérabilités aux changements d'interface utilisateur et proposant l'algorithme TimeTraj qui améliore la généralisation en entraînant sur des trajectoires à travers plusieurs versions de conception.

de.title: TimeWarp: Bewertung von Web-Agenten durch Überprüfung der Vergangenheit
de.summary: TimeWarp-Benchmark testet die Robustheit von Web-Agenten gegenüber sich ändernden Website-Designs über Internet-Epochen hinweg, offenbart Anfälligkeiten für UI-Änderungen und schlägt den TimeTraj-Algorithmus vor, der die Verallgemeinerung durch Training auf Trajektorien über mehrere Design-Versionen hinweg verbessert.

es.title: TimeWarp: Evaluando agentes web revisitando el pasado
es.summary: El benchmark TimeWarp prueba la robustez de los agentes web frente a cambios de diseño de sitios web a lo largo de las eras de Internet, revelando vulnerabilidades a cambios de interfaz de usuario y proponiendo el algoritmo TimeTraj que mejora la generalización al entrenar en trayectorias en múltiples versiones de diseño.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Covariate Time Series
summary: RAG4CTS extends retrieval-augmented generation to time-series foundation models for predictive maintenance scenarios, using physics-informed retrieval and dynamic context optimization, with successful real-world deployment in aviation valve monitoring.
content:
arXiv:2603.04951v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While RAG has greatly enhanced LLMs, extending this paradigm to Time-Series Foundation Models (TSFMs) remains a challenge. This is exemplified in the Predictive Maintenance of the Pressure Regulating and Shut-Off Valve (PRSOV), a high-stakes industrial scenario characterized by (1) data scarcity, (2) short transient sequences, and (3) covariate coupled dynamics. Unfortunately, existing time-series RAG approaches predominantly rely on generated static vector embeddings and learnable context augmenters, which may fail to distinguish similar regimes in such scarce, transient, and covariate coupled scenarios. To address these limitations, we propose RAG4CTS, a regime-aware, training-free RAG framework for Covariate Time-Series. Specifically, we construct a hierarchal time-series native knowledge base to enable lossless storage and physics-informed retrieval of raw historical regimes. We design a two-stage bi-weighted retrieval mechanism that aligns historical trends through point-wise and multivariate similarities. For context augmentation, we introduce an agent-driven strategy to dynamically optimize context in a self-supervised manner. Extensive experiments on PRSOV demonstrate that our framework significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in prediction accuracy. The proposed system is deployed in Apache IoTDB within China Southern Airlines. Since deployment, our method has successfully identified one PRSOV fault in two months with zero false alarm.

zh.title: 基于协变量时间序列的检索增强生成
zh.summary: RAG4CTS将检索增强生成扩展到时间序列基础模型，用于预测性维护场景，采用物理信息驱动的检索和动态上下文优化，在航空阀门监测中成功部署。

fr.title: Génération augmentée par récupération avec série temporelle de covariables
fr.summary: RAG4CTS étend la génération augmentée par récupération aux modèles de fondation de séries temporelles pour les scénarios de maintenance prédictive, en utilisant la récupération informée par la physique et l'optimisation du contexte dynamique, avec un déploiement réussi en surveillance des vannes aéronautiques.

de.title: Retrieval-augmented Generation mit Kovariate-Zeitreihen
de.summary: RAG4CTS erweitert die Retrieval-augmented Generation auf Zeit-Reihen-Grundmodelle für prädiktive Wartungsszenarien, verwendet physikgesteuerte Abfrage und dynamische Kontextoptimierung mit erfolgreicher Echtweltimplementierung in der Luftfahrtventilüberwachung.

es.title: Generación Aumentada por Recuperación con Series Temporales de Covariables
es.summary: RAG4CTS extiende la generación aumentada por recuperación a modelos de fundación de series temporales para escenarios de mantenimiento predictivo, utilizando recuperación informada por física y optimización de contexto dinámico, con despliegue exitoso en monitoreo de válvulas aeronáuticas.

title: Rethinking Representativeness and Diversity in Dynamic Data Selection
summary: A new dynamic data selection framework redefines representativeness and diversity at the process level, using sparse autoencoders to prioritize frequently-occurring features while gradually incorporating rare patterns, achieving 2x training acceleration without sacrificing accuracy.
content:
arXiv:2603.04981v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Dynamic data selection accelerates training by sampling a changing subset of the dataset while preserving accuracy. We rethink two core notions underlying sample evaluation: representativeness and diversity. Instead of local geometric centrality, we define representativeness as coverage of dataset-level common or high-frequency feature factors. Instead of within-subset dispersion, we define diversity at the process level, requiring the selection trajectory to gradually include complementary rare factors over training. Based on this view, we propose a dynamic selection framework with three components. First, we score representativeness in a plug-in feature space to prioritize samples covering frequent factors. We instantiate this with a sparse autoencoder trained on the target dataset, using sparse unit activations to summarize both individual samples and dataset-wide factor statistics. Second, we realize process-level diversity by combining rare-factor sampling with a Usage-Frequency Penalty that promotes sample rotation, provably discourages monopoly, and reduces gradient bias. Third, we couple the two-dimensional scoring with a smooth scheduler that transitions selection from core-pattern consolidation to rare-factor exploration, without extra gradients, influence estimates, or second-order computations on the training model. Extensive experiments on five benchmarks across vision and text tasks demonstrate improved accuracy-efficiency trade-offs across models. Our method matches or exceeds full-data accuracy with over 2x training acceleration. Code will be released.

zh.title: 重新思考动态数据选择中的代表性和多样性
zh.summary: 一个新的动态数据选择框架在过程层级重新定义了代表性和多样性，使用稀疏自编码器优先考虑频繁出现的特征，同时逐步纳入罕见模式，实现了2倍的训练加速而不牺牲准确性。

fr.title: Repenser la représentativité et la diversité dans la sélection dynamique des données
fr.summary: Un nouveau cadre de sélection dynamique des données redéfinit la représentativité et la diversité au niveau du processus, en utilisant des autoencodeurs clairsemés pour prioriser les caractéristiques fréquentes tout en incorporant progressivement des motifs rares, réalisant une accélération 2x de la formation sans sacrifier la précision.

de.title: Überdenken von Repräsentativität und Vielfalt in der dynamischen Datenauswahl
de.summary: Ein neues Framework zur dynamischen Datenauswahl definiert Repräsentativität und Vielfalt auf Prozessebene neu, verwendet spärliche Autoencoder, um häufig vorkommende Merkmale zu priorisieren, während schrittweise seltene Muster einbezogen werden, was eine 2x Trainings-Beschleunigung ohne Genauigkeitsverlust erreicht.

es.title: Repensar la Representatividad y la Diversidad en la Selección Dinámica de Datos
es.summary: Un nuevo marco de selección dinámica de datos redefine la representatividad y la diversidad a nivel de proceso, utilizando autoencodificadores dispersos para priorizar características frecuentes mientras se incorporan gradualmente patrones raros, logrando una aceleración de entrenamiento de 2x sin sacrificar la precisión.

title: BioLLMAgent: A Hybrid Framework with Enhanced Structural Interpretability for Simulating Human Decision-Making in Computational Psychiatry
summary: BioLLMAgent combines reinforcement learning cognitive models with LLMs to simulate human psychiatric decision-making with structural interpretability, validated on gambling and therapy tasks and providing insights for testing psychiatric interventions.
content:
arXiv:2603.05016v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Computational psychiatry faces a fundamental trade-off: traditional reinforcement learning (RL) models offer interpretability but lack behavioral realism, while large language model (LLM) agents generate realistic behaviors but lack structural interpretability. We introduce BioLLMAgent, a novel hybrid framework that combines validated cognitive models with the generative capabilities of LLMs. The framework comprises three core components: (i) an Internal RL Engine for experience-driven value learning; (ii) an External LLM Shell for high-level cognitive strategies and therapeutic interventions; and (iii) a Decision Fusion Mechanism for integrating components via weighted utility. Comprehensive experiments on the Iowa Gambling Task (IGT) across six clinical and healthy datasets demonstrate that BioLLMAgent accurately reproduces human behavioral patterns while maintaining excellent parameter identifiability (correlations $>0.67$). Furthermore, the framework successfully simulates cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) principles and reveals, through multi-agent dynamics, that community-wide educational interventions may outperform individual treatments. Validated across reward-punishment learning and temporal discounting tasks, BioLLMAgent provides a structurally interpretable "computational sandbox" for testing mechanistic hypotheses and intervention strategies in psychiatric research.

zh.title: BioLLMAgent：用于模拟计算精神病学中人类决策的具有增强结构可解释性的混合框架
zh.summary: BioLLMAgent将强化学习认知模型与LLM相结合，以模拟具有结构可解释性的人类精神病学决策制定，在赌博和治疗任务上进行了验证，为测试精神病学干预提供了见解。

fr.title: BioLLMAgent : Un cadre hybride avec interprétabilité structurelle améliorée pour simuler la prise de décision humaine en psychiatrie computationnelle
fr.summary: BioLLMAgent combine les modèles cognitifs d'apprentissage par renforcement avec les LLM pour simuler la prise de décision psychiatrique humaine avec interprétabilité structurelle, validée sur des tâches de jeu et de thérapie, fournissant des perspectives pour tester les interventions psychiatriques.

de.title: BioLLMAgent: Ein Hybrid-Framework mit verbesserter struktureller Interpretierbarkeit zur Simulation der menschlichen Entscheidungsfindung in der rechnergestützten Psychiatrie
de.summary: BioLLMAgent kombiniert Verstärkungslern-Kognitionsmodelle mit LLMs, um menschliche psychiatrische Entscheidungsfindung mit struktureller Interpretierbarkeit zu simulieren, validiert auf Glücksspiel- und Therapieaufgaben und liefert Erkenntnisse zum Testen psychiatrischer Interventionen.

es.title: BioLLMAgent: Un Marco Híbrido con Interpretabilidad Estructural Mejorada para Simular la Toma de Decisiones Humana en Psiquiatría Computacional
es.summary: BioLLMAgent combina modelos cognitivos de aprendizaje por refuerzo con LLMs para simular la toma de decisiones psiquiátricas humanas con interpretabilidad estructural, validado en tareas de apuestas y terapia, proporcionando perspectivas para probar intervenciones psiquiátricas.

title: Measuring the Fragility of Trust: Devising Credibility Index via Explanation Stability (CIES) for Business Decision Support Systems
summary: CIES measures how stable AI explanations remain under realistic data perturbations in business systems, providing practitioners a deployable "credibility warning system" for detecting when explanation reliability degrades due to model complexity or data imbalance.
content:
arXiv:2603.05024v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) methods (SHAP, LIME) are increasingly adopted to interpret models in high-stakes businesses. However, the credibility of these explanations, their stability under realistic data perturbations, remains unquantified. This paper introduces the Credibility Index via Explanation Stability (CIES), a mathematically grounded metric that measures how robust a model's explanations are when subject to realistic business noise. CIES captures whether the reasons behind a prediction remain consistent, not just the prediction itself. The metric employs a rank-weighted distance function that penalizes instability in the most important features disproportionately, reflecting business semantics where changes in top decision drivers are more consequential than changes in marginal features. We evaluate CIES across three datasets (customer churn, credit risk, employee attrition), four tree-based classification models and two data balancing conditions. Results demonstrate that model complexity impacts explanation credibility, class imbalance treatment via SMOTE affects not only predictive performance but also explanation stability, and CIES provides statistically superior discriminative power compared to a uniform baseline metric (p < 0.01 in all 24 configurations). A sensitivity analysis across four noise levels confirms the robustness of the metric itself. These findings offer business practitioners a deployable "credibility warning system" for AI-driven decision support.

zh.title: 衡量信任的脆弱性：通过解释稳定性为商业决策支持系统设计可信度指数（CIES）
zh.summary: CIES衡量人工智能解释在商业系统中现实数据扰动下的稳定性，为从业者提供可部署的"可信度预警系统"，用于检测模型复杂性或数据不平衡导致的解释可靠性下降。

fr.title: Mesurer la fragilité de la confiance : Concevoir un indice de crédibilité via la stabilité des explications (CIES) pour les systèmes d'aide à la décision commerciale
fr.summary: CIES mesure la stabilité des explications de l'IA face aux perturbations réalistes des données dans les systèmes métier, fournissant aux praticiens un système d'avertissement de crédibilité déployable pour détecter quand la fiabilité des explications se dégrade en raison de la complexité du modèle ou du déséquilibre des données.

de.title: Messung der Zerbrechlichkeit von Vertrauen: Entwicklung eines Glaubwürdigkeitsindex durch Erklärungsstabilität (CIES) für Geschäftsentscheidungshilfesysteme
de.summary: CIES misst, wie stabil KI-Erklärungen unter realistischen Datenstörungen in Geschäftssystemen bleiben, und bietet Praktikern ein implementierbares Glaubwürdigkeitswarnsystem zur Erkennung, wann die Zuverlässigkeit von Erklärungen aufgrund von Modellkomplexität oder Datenunausgeglichenheit abnimmt.

es.title: Midiendo la fragilidad de la confianza: Diseñando un índice de credibilidad a través de la estabilidad de las explicaciones (CIES) para sistemas de apoyo a la decisión empresarial
es.summary: CIES mide la estabilidad de las explicaciones de IA ante perturbaciones realistas de datos en sistemas comerciales, proporcionando a los profesionales un sistema de advertencia de credibilidad implementable para detectar cuándo la confiabilidad de las explicaciones se degrada debido a la complejidad del modelo o al desequilibrio de datos.

title: S5-SHB Agent: Society 5.0 enabled Multi-model Agentic Blockchain Framework for Smart Home
summary: S5-SHB-Agent orchestrates multiple LLMs as specialized agents to govern blockchain-based smart homes across safety, security, energy, and privacy domains, with adaptive consensus protocols and resident-controlled governance aligned with Society 5.0 principles.
content:
arXiv:2603.05027v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The smart home is a key application domain within the Society 5.0 vision for a human-centered society. As smart home ecosystems expand with heterogeneous IoT protocols, diverse devices, and evolving threats, autonomous systems must manage comfort, security, energy, and safety for residents.
